primaryConcerns.desc: Your inputs fall within healthy physiological bounds.

recommendations: Maintain your current lifestyle, including diet and exercise habits.
Schedule an annual check-up to monitor baseline vitals.
Ensure adequate sleep (7-9 hours/night) for proper recovery.

primaryConcerns.desc: Elevated BMI strongly correlates with insulin resistance.
Smoking drastically restricts blood flow and damages blood vessels.

recommendations: Focus on a balanced diet to reduce BMI to a healthy range.
Consider a smoking cessation program to lower cardiovascular risk immediately.

primaryConcerns.desc: Elevated BMI strongly correlates with insulin resistance.

primaryConcerns.desc: Heart rate and ECG changes can indicate rhythm irregularities that need clinical review.
Raised temperature, especially with fast heart rate or low oxygen, can suggest infection or inflammation.
BMI, smoking, activity level, and heart-rate strain are combined into a cardiovascular risk estimate.
Low oxygen saturation is associated with breathing difficulty and reduced oxygen delivery.
Smoking history and oxygen saturation are used to estimate chronic respiratory risk.
High temperature and fast heart rate can occur with heat stress or dehydration.
Weight, inactivity, and alcohol intake can cluster with metabolic risk factors.
Elevated BMI and low activity are commonly associated with insulin resistance.
Higher BMI and oxygen dips can be associated with sleep-related breathing problems.
Low oxygen readings with heart-rate changes can suggest reduced oxygen delivery.
Alcohol intake and obesity can increase liver strain risk.
Low body temperature can affect heart rhythm and circulation.

recommendations: Use these results as a screening estimate, not a medical diagnosis.
Repeat the measurement after sitting still for a few minutes to improve sensor reliability.
If oxygen saturation remains below 95% or breathing feels difficult, seek medical advice.
Monitor temperature, hydration, and symptoms if fever persists.
Recheck heart rate at rest and consult a clinician if abnormal values continue.

primaryConcerns.disease: ECG Lead-Off or Sensor Contact Issue
primaryConcerns.desc: Lead-off flags mean the ECG electrodes may not be attached properly, so ECG-based disease risk is less reliable.
Smoking history and oxygen saturation are used to estimate chronic respiratory risk.
